title: The Roar That Was Speech

features.name: unhearable-frequency
features.desc-on-touch: A tone that only appears when the recording is reversed. The archive keeps both the forward and backward versions. Neither is the truth.

features.name: merged-river-voices
features.desc-on-touch: You're reading where sentence-rivers stopped being words and became the sound of water remembering it once held meaning.

features.name: transcription-failure
features.desc-on-touch: A page of notes where the researcher gave up. The handwriting stops mid-word. The archive files this as data: sometimes silence is what was meant to be heard.

features.name: the-roar-itself
features.desc-on-touch: A monumental acoustic event: all words colliding at the point where they gave up being language. The archive records the pressure of sound.

features.name: echo-catalogue
features.desc-on-touch: Every roar logged with the coordinates of where it was heard. Each echo gets its own entry. The archive cannot decide if these are different roars or one long one.

inscription: where words end, the roar begins; where the roar ends, something else continues without a name

file.name: Phonograph_Corruption_Field_Notes_Reverse_Sequence.mp3
file.note: Recording of river-mouth at dusk. When played backward, voices emerge that were not audible forward. Archive position: both versions are canonical.

exits.label: upstream toward the word-river's mouth where speech still remembers itself
downward into the ocean where all roars have the same voice
back through the confluence where words were still attempting meaning
toward the distant listening station that has never reported what it heard
